jokes.text: What do you mean, you can't? Look... I am throwing a farewell party for my best friend, Ann Perkins. And if she casually mentioned 3 years ago that she thought indoor fireworks would be cool then guess what, you're gonna give me indoor fireworks.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to get indoor fireworks for Ann's party
jokes.punchline: The absurd level of detail Leslie remembers and her aggressive determination
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie Knope character moment - her obsessive friendship devotion and ability to remember tiny details from years ago. The escalating tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: That promise launched a friendship so grand it takes 103 scrapbooks to capture it.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining her friendship with Ann
jokes.punchline: The ridiculously specific and excessive number of scrapbooks
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie over-documentation. The specific number '103' makes it funnier than just 'lots of scrapbooks.'

jokes.text: Damn, that's really good. Right? There's gonna be a lot of these at the party. You should come by. I kno-- I get it. I know what you're doing. And I still--is there a little crunch in there? Hazelnut. Ooh. Ooh, right? See you tonight.
jokes.setup: Harold is angry at Leslie but she offers him a treat
jokes.punchline: Harold gets completely distracted by how delicious the treat is despite trying to stay mad
jokes.explanation: Good escalating comedy as Harold's anger completely dissolves due to the treat. The repeated 'Ooh' reactions sell it.

jokes.text: April: Ann's leaving town and saying painful good-byes to loved ones. Greatest day of my life!
jokes.setup: April describing Ann's emotional departure
jokes.punchline: April's genuine excitement about Ann leaving
jokes.explanation: Perfect April moment - the stark contrast between the 'painful good-byes' setup and her genuine joy is expertly delivered. Maximum economy and character integration.

jokes.text: This one has plates' name on it.
jokes.setup: Characters finding boxes with their names
jokes.punchline: Ron's literal interpretation of a box labeled for plates
jokes.explanation: Classic Ron deadpan literalism, very efficient delivery

jokes.text: Fun pun alert.
jokes.setup: Leslie about to make a pun
jokes.punchline: She literally announces it's a pun before making it
jokes.explanation: Leslie's enthusiastic meta-commentary on her own wordplay is very in character. Short and sweet.

jokes.text: I would have preferred a regular hamburger for eating, but this is very nice.
jokes.setup: Leslie gives Ron a bronzed hamburger as a memento
jokes.punchline: Ron's practical response about preferring an edible hamburger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron deadpan - he's polite but can't help pointing out the impractical nature of a bronzed hamburger.

jokes.text: 2008 Indiana state audit spreadsheets? Man, that year was insane! That was the year you went from being an auditor to a legend.
jokes.setup: Ben receives audit spreadsheets as a gift
jokes.punchline: Leslie's over-the-top enthusiasm about boring spreadsheets and calling Ben a 'legend' for auditing
jokes.explanation: Great Leslie moment - she finds genuine excitement in the most mundane government work. The 'auditor to legend' line is perfect.

jokes.text: A picture of me officiating Leslie and Ben's wedding. And look, you can just about see my head.
jokes.setup: Tom describing a photo from Leslie and Ben's wedding
jokes.punchline: He's proud that you can barely see his head in the photo
jokes.explanation: Tom's self-importance meets reality - he's genuinely excited about being barely visible in a photo.

jokes.text: I did have to go to a very seedy location to find it, but now I know where to go to place a bet on horse fighting.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining how she got illegal Snakejuice
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning she discovered horse fighting betting in the process
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - starts with finding banned alcohol, ends with discovering bizarre underground gambling. 'Horse fighting' is wonderfully absurd.

jokes.text: Tell Ann about the groundbreaking, frame Ann for murder, release a deadly virus into the water supply.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks Tom to repeat back what NOT to tell Ann
jokes.punchline: Tom lists increasingly insane things he shouldn't do
jokes.explanation: Good escalating absurdity. Goes from party spoiler to murder to bioterrorism in a natural progression.

jokes.text: Why is there a New Year's Eve countdown clock and the Easter bunny? I don't know when we're gonna be able to visit each other, so just to be safe this party is a celebration of every event that's gonna happen this year.
jokes.setup: Ann notices random holiday decorations at her farewell party
jokes.punchline: Leslie's logic that since they might not see each other, she's celebrating every possible holiday at once
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie over-planning logic. The visual of mixing New Year's and Easter is absurd, and her reasoning is sweetly ridiculous.

jokes.text: Ain't no party like a Leslie Knope party 'cause a Leslie Knope party is actually 30 parties.
jokes.setup: Riff on the classic 'ain't no party like an S Club party' line
jokes.punchline: Leslie's parties are literally multiple parties combined
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that perfectly captures Leslie's over-the-top party planning. Very quotable and character-specific.

jokes.text: This is just a bunch of bras and knives. And loose onions.
jokes.setup: Leslie packed a bag for Ann without looking
jokes.punchline: The completely random and impractical combination of items
jokes.explanation: The randomness is perfect - bras and knives are both necessities but terrible together, and 'loose onions' is wonderfully specific and useless.

jokes.text: We have been on the prowl for some amazing pots. Well, that card's for three pans.
jokes.setup: Gift card from 'Pots and Pans' store
jokes.punchline: Andy was hunting for pots but the card is only for pans
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay on the store name. Andy's enthusiasm about pot-hunting contrasted with the practical pan limitation.

jokes.text: Carob cookies and berries are literally my favorite dessert alternative.
jokes.setup: Chris's corner of the party has health food
jokes.punchline: Chris is genuinely excited about the blandest possible dessert
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris Traeger moment - his enthusiasm for healthy but flavorless food. 'Literally' usage is spot-on for the character.

jokes.text: I also shook his hand. Twice. Anything more than that would be excessive.
jokes.setup: Discussion about getting Chris a better gift
jokes.punchline: Ron thinks two handshakes is already generous friendship
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Ron Swanson moment. The idea that two handshakes represents excessive emotional investment is perfectly in character and hilarious.

jokes.text: I say we start with the three 'C's,' cashmere, concert tickets, caboodles of cash. I feel like those are perfect gifts for you. Those are perfect gifts for anyone.
jokes.setup: Tom suggesting gift ideas for Chris
jokes.punchline: Ann pointing out Tom is just listing things he wants, Tom agreeing
jokes.explanation: Good Tom character moment - his selfishness in gift-giving exposed, but he's completely unapologetic about it.

jokes.text: There's also a sign that says 'Especially Leslie Knope.' Well, Leslie Knope is a very common name. There's a professor in Copenhagen named Leslie Knope. They probably mean him.
jokes.setup: Harold put up a specific sign banning Leslie Knope
jokes.punchline: Leslie's ridiculous attempt to pretend it's not about her
jokes.explanation: Leslie's elaborate denial is funny, especially the specificity of 'professor in Copenhagen.' Good escalation with the photo reveal.

jokes.text: Holy mother of Malia! And Sasha. I love them both equally.
jokes.setup: Leslie expressing shock
jokes.punchline: Correcting herself to include both Obama daughters equally
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie political correctness moment - even in her exclamations she has to be fair to both Obama daughters.

jokes.text: Leslie can't watch anything with Julie Andrews in it before bedtime because it gets her too hyper.
jokes.setup: Ben giving Ann tips about Leslie's quirks
jokes.punchline: Leslie gets overstimulated by wholesome musicals
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Leslie's intense enthusiasm - even Mary Poppins is too stimulating for bedtime. Very specific and believable character detail.

jokes.text: If Chris gets cranky around noon, just stuff some chia seeds into a fig--works every time.
jokes.setup: Ann sharing Chris care instructions
jokes.punchline: The weirdly specific health food solution to mood problems
jokes.explanation: Great Chris Traeger characterization through Ann's knowledge of his health obsessions. The specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I told him that One Headlight By the Wallflowers isn't 'dancing' music and he said 'Not with that attitude.'
jokes.setup: Ann explaining where Chris is
jokes.punchline: Chris's relentless positivity applied to inappropriate dance music
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris moment - his optimism overriding reality. 'One Headlight' is famously melancholy, making his response perfect.

jokes.text: Dude, Ann, you know what I just remembered? We used to date. Yeah, we lived together for two years. I know, it's crazy, right? Anyways, you're the best. Have fun in Mexico.
jokes.setup: Andy having a realization about Ann
jokes.punchline: He casually 'remembers' they dated for two years, then immediately moves on
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Andy moment - treating a two-year relationship as a casual 'oh yeah' memory perfectly captures his character. The quick subject change is gold.

jokes.text: I'm deleting you from my phone. You're finally free of my animal magnetism. Maybe one day years from now when we're both old and Chris is dead, you'll run into me somewhere. I'll be opening my latest mega club. I'll see you there on the street by yourself and I'll tell the bouncer/my bodyguard, 'Hey, that woman's way too old to get into this club, but why don't you let her in the back?'
jokes.setup: Tom's farewell to Ann
jokes.punchline: Elaborate fantasy that gets increasingly insulting and self-aggrandizing
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom - starts romantic, becomes insulting while still thinking he's being generous. The escalating details make it funnier.

jokes.text: Good-bye, Ann. I have enjoyed parts of our time together.
jokes.setup: Ron's farewell to Ann
jokes.punchline: Qualified endorsement - only 'parts' of their time together
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron - even his kindest farewell is carefully qualified. Maximum economy and character integration.

jokes.text: This is my contribution. It says 'Ron.'
jokes.setup: Ron's gift for Chris
jokes.punchline: Just his name written down, presented as if it's meaningful
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly minimalist Ron contribution. The deadpan delivery of this non-gift is perfect.

jokes.text: Dark forces, arise! Okay, I didn't plan what we were all gonna say. That's my bad.
jokes.setup: Group chant before the groundbreaking mission
jokes.punchline: April's evil chant followed by Leslie's acknowledgment that she didn't coordinate
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between April's darkness and Leslie's organizational oversight. Meta-humor about the awkward chant.

jokes.text: Nothing gross. Okay, yeah, there is something I want. Super fizzy fruity pop.
jokes.setup: Harold can have anything he wants as payment
jokes.punchline: His mundane desire for office soda after the 'nothing gross' caveat
jokes.explanation: Good anticlimax - builds up to 'anything you want' then deflates to office beverages. The 'nothing gross' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Kathryn Pinewood is the person on earth who hates me the most. Well, luckily, you're with the person on earth who loves you the most. Too cheesy? No, it's perfect.
jokes.setup: Leslie worried about facing her enemy
jokes.punchline: Ann's sweet response followed by Leslie's meta-commentary on her own cheesiness
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment with good meta-humor. Leslie's awareness of her own sentiment is endearing.

jokes.text: April-- Yeah, you saw a lot of potential in me, I'm like a daughter to you, and it was a treat to watch me blossom. Blah blah blah, bye. That is remarkably close to what I was going to say. Also, I'm proud of you.
jokes.setup: Ann tries to give April a sentimental goodbye
jokes.punchline: April sarcastically predicts exactly what Ann will say, Ann admits it's accurate
jokes.explanation: Excellent character dynamic - April's cynicism meets Ann's sincerity, but Ann's honesty disarms the cynicism.

jokes.text: Donna, are you grabbing my butt? Can you blame me? No!
jokes.setup: Emotional goodbye moment
jokes.punchline: Donna takes advantage of the hug to grab Ann's butt
jokes.explanation: Very Donna behavior - opportunistic and unapologetic. Ann's resigned acceptance makes it work.

jokes.text: I don't have time for disgraced former politicians and unmarried preggos.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ann ask Kathryn for help
jokes.punchline: Kathryn's brutal dismissal of both women
jokes.explanation: Efficiently cruel Kathryn line - manages to insult both women simultaneously with specific references.

jokes.text: What the hell? Yeah, Pinewood, that's right. I'm pregnant and I have you in a headlock. And if you try to fight back, I will sue you.
jokes.setup: Kathryn insults Ann and Leslie
jokes.punchline: Pregnant Ann puts Kathryn in a headlock and threatens legal action
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - sweet Ann snapping into action. The logic of using pregnancy as legal protection is brilliant.

jokes.text: When is this town gonna realize that I'm more than a former high school basketball star? I don't think this new sports drink is even about basketball. It's called 'Sweetums dunktastic three-point B-ball blast' the slogan is 'It's all about basketball.'
jokes.setup: Detlef complains about being typecast as a basketball player
jokes.punchline: The drink is extremely basketball-themed despite his denial
jokes.explanation: Good irony - the product name completely contradicts his claim that it's not about basketball.

jokes.text: There is such a thing as journalistic integrity, and it is something that I have as a journalist with integrity. Well, the story of that appeal is it was heartfelt. And in response to that appeal, I respond: Okay.
jokes.setup: Perd stands firm on journalistic principles
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandons them after one heartfelt plea
jokes.explanation: Perfect Perd - overly formal language about integrity followed by immediate capitulation. His speech pattern is nailed.

jokes.text: For old time's sake? Does that mea--no! Ann, you tricky minx. Yep. I went on a date with Perd. What? It was during that 'explore my freedom as a single woman' phase.
jokes.setup: Perd agrees 'for old time's sake'
jokes.punchline: Revelation that Ann dated Perd, much to Leslie's shock
jokes.explanation: Excellent reveal - Ann's secret dating life continues to surprise. Leslie's reaction sells it perfectly.

jokes.text: And at the end he said, 'I am going to kiss you now by putting my mouth on your mouth.' And then I ran away.
jokes.setup: Ann describing her date with Perd
jokes.punchline: Perd's overly literal description of kissing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Perd characterization - his news anchor formality applied to romantic situations. Extremely quotable and cringe-funny.

jokes.text: We tell him we had the perfect gift, but we were robbed at gunpoint. 'Chris, he had a gun. He took all the gifts.' Now Chris feels bad, he's buying us gifts. Next thing you know, Tommy's got a new watch.
jokes.setup: Tom's alternative to telling Chris the truth
jokes.punchline: Elaborate lie that somehow results in them getting gifts instead
jokes.explanation: Classic Tom scheme - overly complex plan to benefit himself. The specific 'Tommy's got a new watch' detail is perfect.

jokes.text: Ask not what your country can do for you. From Family Guy, right?
jokes.setup: Andy quotes JFK
jokes.punchline: He thinks it's from Family Guy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy ignorance - attributing a famous presidential quote to a cartoon. Short and effective.

jokes.text: And just the fact that you feel bad about your bag of nothing proves that.
jokes.setup: Chris reassuring the group about their gifts
jokes.punchline: He calls their terrible gift a 'bag of nothing' but in a positive way
jokes.explanation: Chris's relentless positivity turning their failure into a virtue. Good callback to earlier gift-giving.

jokes.text: You two are a couple of real pains in the ass. You know that? Harold, your tiny brain could not understand this, but that is the best compliment you could ever give the two women standing in front of you.
jokes.setup: Harold calls Leslie and Ann pains in the ass
jokes.punchline: Leslie takes it as the ultimate compliment
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment - turning an insult into pride. The 'tiny brain' insult back at Harold works well.

jokes.text: Harold, your tiny brain could not understand this, but that is the best compliment you could ever give the two women standing in front of you.
jokes.setup: Harold calling Leslie and Ann pains in the ass
jokes.punchline: Leslie treating it as the highest compliment while insulting Harold
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment turning insult into badge of honor while getting in her own dig. Solid character work.

jokes.text: It's for the raccoons. You have to hiss really loud and stomp your feet so they'll leave you alone. And if one comes close, make yourself really large like a puffer fish.
jokes.setup: Ann asks what April is doing
jokes.punchline: April's elaborate raccoon defense strategy involving puffer fish tactics
jokes.explanation: Great April weirdness - treating raccoons like dangerous predators with marine biology tactics.

jokes.text: Well, Michigan has wolverines. Maybe they'll eat your toes.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Pawnee vs Michigan
jokes.punchline: April's morbid hope that wolverines will harm Ann
jokes.explanation: Perfect April darkness - casually wishing violence on Ann but in a specific, almost cute way.

jokes.text: I made Andy promise that someday we'll move to Transylvania, but that probably won't happen till we're, like, 100.
jokes.setup: Discussion of future plans
jokes.punchline: April's goal to move to the vampire homeland when elderly
jokes.explanation: Perfect April gothic aspirations. The casual timeline planning for vampire relocation is wonderfully weird.

jokes.text: You have to hiss really loud and stomp your feet so they'll leave you alone. And if one comes close, make yourself really large like a puffer fish.
jokes.setup: April explaining how to deal with raccoons
jokes.punchline: The bizarre combination of animal defense strategies
jokes.explanation: Good April weirdness - mixing legitimate and absurd animal defense strategies. The puffer fish comparison is perfectly unexpected.

jokes.text: My going-away present to you will be that I do not make you audibly say that you love me. Thank you.
jokes.setup: April quietly admits she loves Ann
jokes.punchline: Ann's merciful decision not to force April to repeat it out loud
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment - Ann understands April well enough to not force emotional vulnerability. Perfect friendship dynamics.

jokes.text: I call it my 'beat the sun' run. It's a race between me and the sun.
jokes.setup: Chris explaining his morning routine
jokes.punchline: He literally races against the sun rising
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris fitness obsession - turning dawn into a competitive event. Very in character.

jokes.text: It's quarter-sawn zebra wood adjoined by floating tenons to the black walnut corner posts. Finished with a wiping varnish that's a secret Swanson family recipe.
jokes.setup: Ron describing the craftsmanship of his gift
jokes.punchline: The incredibly detailed technical woodworking description
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron craftsmanship obsession - the technical detail is overwhelming and authentically passionate for his character.

jokes.text: That's Garry Jerry Larry Gergich Gengurch. I burned in all my names. I really--I didn't know what to do.
jokes.setup: Mystery initials on the baby box
jokes.punchline: Larry's confusion about which of his many names to use
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the ongoing name confusion. Larry's genuine bewilderment makes it work.

jokes.text: That's Garry Jerry Larry Gergich Gengurch. I burned in all my names. I really--I didn't know what to do.
jokes.setup: Someone asking whose initials G-J-L-G-G are on the box
jokes.punchline: Jerry's confusion about his multiple names and burning all of them
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Jerry's name problems throughout the series. His genuine confusion and over-preparation is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: This tear, caused by the overwhelming thoughtfulness of my friends will be my baby's first memory. Salt water will warp the wood. So keep your tears in your eyes where they belong.
jokes.setup: Ann getting emotional about the thoughtful gift
jokes.punchline: Ron's practical concern about tears damaging his craftsmanship
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - emotional sentiment immediately undercut by practical woodworking concerns. Great contrast.

jokes.text: But most importantly, you taught me that I can't pull off a tulip skirt. It's just not your shape.
jokes.setup: Leslie listing what Ann taught her
jokes.punchline: Fashion advice as the most important life lesson
jokes.explanation: Good mix of sentiment and practical fashion reality. Ann's blunt honesty about Leslie's style choices.

jokes.text: All right, I got my park. That's all I wanted from you. Friendship over. Don't even joke about that, Ann.
jokes.setup: Ann jokingly says their friendship is over now that the park is built
jokes.punchline: Leslie's immediate panic at the suggestion
jokes.explanation: Sweet moment showing Leslie's genuine attachment despite Ann's joke. Good emotional beat.

jokes.text: There's a lot of Sarah McLanahan, so, Chris, I apologize. Things are gonna get emotional.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing the road trip mix CD
jokes.punchline: Warning Chris about the emotional impact of Sarah McLanahan music
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie thoughtfulness mixed with awareness of Sarah McLanahan's reputation for sad music.

jokes.text: Shotgun! I called shotgun! You're all nailed. I call sitting on your lap. You can't--I--double shotgun! We call double shotgun! How come people don't do that more often? It's illegal.
jokes.setup: Everyone fighting for shotgun in the car
jokes.punchline: Escalating shotgun calls ending with Ron's practical observation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation comedy with multiple characters piling on, ended by Ron's matter-of-fact legal knowledge.

jokes.text: There has never been a sadness that can't be cured by breakfast food.
jokes.setup: Ron offering to take sad Leslie to breakfast
jokes.punchline: His philosophy about breakfast food's healing powers
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron Swanson food philosophy - treating breakfast as universal medicine. Good quotable wisdom that fits his character.
